message:
fix(health): distinguish recovering from corrupt in /health body

Previously /health mapped both DurabilityState::Recovering and
DurabilityState::Corrupt to 503 with the same body status="degraded".
The HTTP status is correct for both — per the durability state
machine, `allows_reads()` is false for both, so a client routed to a
recovering or corrupt backend genuinely cannot read — but collapsing
them into a single "degraded" body discards useful information:

  - Recovering is a self-healing transient state (active recovery in
    progress; should return to Healthy without operator intervention).
  - Corrupt is a terminal state requiring `am doctor repair` /
    `am doctor reconstruct` by the operator.

Supervisors that want to alert-escalate on Corrupt but ignore
Recovering (knowing the pod will fix itself) previously had to scrape
logs or poll /health/durability to distinguish them. Now the body
status field carries "recovering" vs "degraded" while the HTTP
status stays 503 for both, so k8s / load balancers keep their
existing behavior but Prometheus-style scrapers can react
differently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
